bids.reasoning: At time fraction 0.0, it is beneficial for me to open with an offer that maximizes my utility. The opponent's initial offer proposed the minimum possible utility for me across all issues, indicating they are likely anchoring to their own maximum utility scenario. I will respond by anchoring the negotiation with my ideal outcome on all issues.

In the very early stages (t=0.02) of this negotiation, the opponent is repeating an offer that gives me minimal utility on all fronts. I will make a minor concession on issueC (lowering slightly from my optimal valueA to valueB) to demonstrate flexibility and elicit a more constructive response, without significantly reducing my overall utility.

The opponent is holding firm on their initial offer of 'valueF' for all issues. Since it is very early in the negotiation (t=0.04), I will maintain my high-utility demands but explore concessions across different issues to identify their priorities. I am shifting my concession from Issue C to Issue B to present a new package while keeping my total expected utility above 0.80.

The opponent is holding extremely firm on their initial offer of all 'valueF's at t=0.06. Since it is still very early in the negotiation, I will probe their preferences by proposing a slight concession on issueA to 'valueB' while keeping all other issues at my maximum utility. This still maintains an excellent utility for me (over 90% of max) while adding variation to see if they yield on any front.

Early in the negotiation (t=0.08), the opponent is holding completely firm on a single configuration (valueF for all issues) which yields my minimum possible utility. Recognizing this, I will systematically explore small concessions across different dimensions to identify their actual priorities. Having tested concessions on issues A, B, and C previously, I will now concede slightly on issueD (to valueC) while keeping my other issues at maximum utility, maintaining an overall highly favorable outcome for myself.

The negotiation is still in the early stage (t=0.1) and the opponent is continuously offering their initial bid (valueF across all issues), taking an unyielding stance. I will maintain a firm approach to protect my primary interests, especially on issueA where my weight is highest. However, to keep the negotiation moving, I will introduce a minor concession on issueB, offering valueB, to signal a willingness to find a compromise while expecting some reciprocal movement from the opponent.

The current time fraction is very early at 0.12, so there is no rush to concede substantially. The opponent has held firm on an offer that yields minimal utility for me across all issues (valueF everywhere), meaning they are likely just trying to establish a tough baseline. I will maintain a high-utility posture by keeping my most important issues at maximum utility and proposing a slight concession on issueC to see if they are willing to shift from their immovable stance.

I am still early in the negotiation (t=0.14). The opponent has been stubbornly bidding the exact same values for all issues without variation, providing no signal of their preferences other than a hardline tactic. I will continue making minor concessions on individual issues from my optimal bid in order to elicit a meaningful counter-offer, this time offering 'valueC' on 'issueC' while maintaining my highest utilities on all other issues.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on their starting offer of valueF for all issues, maintaining an inflexible stance. As it is still very early in the negotiation (t=0.16), I will make a minor concession on issueD while holding firm on my most important issue (issueA) and the remaining issues to explore the opponent's willingness to compromise without sacrificing too much utility. This offer targets approximately 88% normalized utility.

I notice the opponent continues to stubbornly propose their initial offer with valueF for all issues, which provides minimal utility to me. Since we are still early in the negotiation (t=0.18), I will maintain a primarily self-interested stance but offer an alternative combination of minor concessions to probe for any flexibility on their end. I am slightly conceding on issueB and issueC while holding firm on my most important issue (issueA) and issueD to protect my overall utility.

The opponent has repeatedly offered the exact same combination (`valueF` for all issues) from t=0.00 to t=0.20, showing no willingness to concede. As the time fraction is still early (t=0.2), I will maintain a firm stance while making minor exploratory concessions to see if they react to different value combinations. I am proposing a combination that yields high utility for me, altering my stance slightly on `issueA` and `issueC` to invite a counteroffer.

The opponent has rigidly maintained the identical offer (valueF for all issues) throughout the negotiation, which offers me minimal utility. Given that we are still early in the process (t=0.22), it is prudent to continue signaling flexibility by exploring different permutations of moderately concessive bids in hopes of finding a baseline they are willing to engage with. I will offer a combination slightly lowering my demands on issueA and issueB while keeping issueC and issueD at valueA to maintain high overall utility.

The opponent has rigidly maintained the exact same opening offer (all valueF) without any concessions or messaging since the start. Since we are still early in the negotiation (t=0.24), I will avoid dropping my utility too quickly but will continue exploring combinations. I am maintaining my highest-priority requirement on issueA while making a minor concession on issueB to test if they are receptive to movement on that issue.

The opponent is holding extremely firm, repeating their initial offer granting them maximum utility while offering me minimal utility. Since it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.26), I will make a very slight concession to explore their preferences without giving up too much ground, targeting a utility of around 0.85 (normalized). I will hold my most important issue (A) and another issue (B) at my maximum utility and slightly concede on C and D to find potential trade-offs.

The opponent continues to repeat their heavily lopsided initial offer (all valueF) without conceding any ground. Given that we are still relatively early in the negotiation (t=0.28), I will maintain a firm stance on my most valuable issues (issueA and issueD in this bid) while making minor exploratory concessions on issueB and issueC to see if they prefer different tradeoffs. This bid targets a high utility (>85% of my maximum) to protect my baseline while offering an alternative configuration.

The opponent has repeatedly sent the exact same opening offer consisting of my lowest-utility values, indicating extreme stubbornness or testing my boundaries. Given that we are still relatively early in the negotiation (t=0.3), I will maintain a high target utility around 0.75. Since I have no feedback on the opponent's true preferences besides their opening anchor, I will continue proposing different combinations to see if they have secondary preferences they might be willing to pivot towards.

The opponent continues to repeat their opening offer consisting completely of their most preferred values, which provide very low utility for me. Since we are still in the early phase of the negotiation (t=0.32), I will maintain my highest value on my most important issue (issueA) while proposing another combination involving slight concessions on issues B and C. This explores potential compromises without needlessly sacrificing significant value early on.

The opponent is holding firm on a single offer that provides me with very low utility. Given that the time fraction is only 0.34, I will not make drastic concessions. I will instead offer a new package that explores tradeoffs among my less critical values while maintaining a high overall utility for myself, hoping to encourage the opponent to finally negotiate.

The opponent has rigidly maintained their opening bid of valueF across all issues. Instead of making uniform marginal concessions, I will strategically concede on one issue entirely to their requested value to see if it breaks the deadlock, while keeping my most important issues at their maximum utility values. I offer valueF for issueD, which gives me reasonable utility, while keeping issueA, issueB, and issueC at valueA.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on their opening exact offer of 'valueF' for all issues, making zero concessions up to t=0.38. Since 'valueF' yields a near-zero utility for me across the board, I cannot accept their proposal. I will continue making moderate concessions down to the ~0.78 utility range by offering new variations on mid-value combos (e.g., issueB=valueC and issueC=valueB) to search for acceptable tradeoffs while maintaining a strong stance on my most critical priority, issueA.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on an offer that maximizes their utility with valueF across all issues without yielding. Since the time fraction is 0.40, I will continue making slight but varied concessions to explore if they are willing to accept trade-offs without sacrificing my higher-weighted issues significantly. I will try conceding moderately on issueA and issueC while holding firm on issueB and issueD, maintaining a targeted utility above 0.75.

The opponent continues to hold firm with their maximum-utility offer (valueF for all issues). Since we are nearing the midpoint (t=0.42), I will maintain a reasonably high utility for myself while attempting another varied combination to discover partial concessions the opponent might accept. I will probe conceding slightly on issueA and issueD while keeping my top requests on issueB and issueC.

The opponent has stubbornly repeated the exact same bid for 23 rounds with no concessions or messages, which would give me a terrible utility (around 0.23). As we approach the halfway mark (t=0.44), I will continue to hold firm on my most important issue (issueA) while offering a new, slightly downgraded combination on the remaining issues (issueB and issueC) to see if it sparks any flexibility, maintaining an overall utility over 0.75.

The opponent has stubbornly maintained the exact same offer yielding minimum utility for me since the beginning. Since we are at t=0.46, I will continue systematically varying values on issues B, C, and D, stepping down slightly on my second and third tier choices to find any acceptable trade-offs for them without compromising on my most important issue A.

I am at time 0.48 and the opponent has repeated the exact same baseline offer (all valueF) since the beginning without any messages. To find an acceptable trade-off while protecting my utility, I will continue making modest concessions to explore which issues the opponent might value most. I will propose an offer that reduces my utility on issueA and issueC slightly, maintaining an overall strong utility for myself (~0.78/0.9) to see if they finally break their stonewalling.

The opponent has stubbornly refused to budge from their opening offer (always bidding valueF across all issues) despite my repeated moderate concessions. Since we are halfway to the deadline (t=0.5), I will make another combination of moderate compromises across issues B and C to signal further flexibility and attempt to break the deadlock without abandoning my baseline utility targets yet.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on their initial offer of 'valueF' for all issues, making absolutely no concessions so far. Since we are just past the midpoint (t=0.52), I will continue to explore different permutations to test if the opponent will respond to specific concessions on individual issues. I will offer a newly untested option on issueC (valueE) while keeping my highest-utility values on the other issues to maintain an overall utility around 0.79.

The opponent has rigidly bid valueF across all issues until now, when they finally conceded on issueD to valueE. However, my utility for issueD: valueE (0.20) is strictly worse than issueD: valueF (0.35). I will capitalize on their apparent preference for valueF on issueD by giving it to them, while slightly softening my demand on issueC to offer a balanced compromise while holding firm on my most valuable issues, issueA and issueB.

The opponent has rigidly bid valueF for almost all issues, briefly varying issueD to valueE before reverting. Since time is passing the midpoint (t=0.56), I will make a substantial exploratory concession to test their priorities. By giving them valueF on issueB while securing high utility on issueA and issueD, I maintain a target utility of ~0.71 and signal a willingness to find a mutually acceptable tradeoff.

The opponent is holding extremely firm on their (valueF) offer across all issues, with only a very minor deviation at t=0.54. Because time is advancing past the midpoint (t=0.58), I need to explore different tradeoff spaces to entice the opponent while still preserving my own utility. I am offering a mix of my second-best values (valueB) on issues A, B, and C, while keeping issue D at my highest value. This ensures a utility of roughly 0.71.

The opponent is consistently stonewalling with the exact same offer (`valueF` for all issues), showing no willingness to compromise, except for a momentary deviation to `issueD: valueE`. To break the deadlock, I'll attempt a mixed bid across different suboptimal values that maintains roughly 0.66 utility for me, effectively probing for which issues they might accept concessions on without dropping my own utility prematurely.

The opponent is holding extremely firm, primarily bidding "valueF" on all issues. However, in their latest offer, they slightly adjusted `issueD` to "valueE". To find a possible avenue for agreement without sacrificing too much utility, I will concede on `issueD` to match their "valueE", while maintaining my best values for `issueA` and `issueB` and a high-utility value for `issueC`. This maintains a high overall utility for myself (~0.70) while probing whether meeting them on `issueD` opens the door for a compromise.

The opponent is holding extremely firm but has recently made a minor concession on issue D from value F to value E. Since we have passed the midpoint of the negotiation, I will match their concession on issue D (value E) to encourage flexibility. I will hold firm on my highest-priority issue A (value A) but concede moderately on issue B to offer a balanced package with a utility around 0.66.

The opponent has finally started conceding, shifting their position on issueC to valueC and issueD to valueE. I will incorporate both of these opponent-selected values to encourage agreement, as they offer me acceptable utility (especially valueC for issueC). However, to keep my overall utility target high since we still have time (t=0.66), I will hold firm on valueA for my highest-weighted issues, issueA and issueB.

The opponent has recently conceded to `valueC` for `issueC` and has persistently offered `valueF` for `issueD`. I am incorporating these two opponent-friendly values into my next offer. By doing so, I hope to capitalize on their concessions while firmly holding my highest-value options on my most critical issues (`issueA` and `issueB`) to maintain a strong overall utility.

The opponent has rigidly held firm on issueA and issueB (valueF), while showing slight flexibility on issueC (valueC) and issueD (valueE/valueF). At t=0.7, advancing towards a compromise is necessary. I will offer their preferred values on issueC and issueD (valueC and valueF, respectively), while making a concession on issueB to valueC to signal my willingness to meet them half-way, while holding firm on heavily weighted issueA.

The opponent has rigidly held out for valueF on issues A and B, but has shown willingness to concede issueC to valueC and issueD to valueE in recent offers. To make progress as time moves past 0.7, I will accept their proposed values of valueC for issueC and valueE for issueD. However, I will maintain valueA on issueA (my most important issue) and concede slightly on issueB to valueD, maintaining an overall utility of ~0.58.

The opponent has signaled their target values by consistently returning to or introducing `issueB: valueF`, `issueC: valueC`, and `issueD: valueE` in recent turns. Since we are in the late stage at `t=0.74`, I will offer exactly these three values to align with their preferences and foster an agreement. To maintain my utility above 0.5, I will hold firm on my top-priority issue (`issueA`) at my highest value.

The opponent has shown flexibility recently on issueA (valueD), issueC (valueC), and issueD (valueE/valueF), although they just reverted to their initial position. Given we are at t=0.76, I need to begin making more concrete trade-offs. I will propose an offer that gives the opponent their preferred value for issueD and a value they recently conceded to for issueC, while slightly dropping my issueA request to valueB to maintain a utility above 0.64.

The negotiation is entering the late stage at t=0.78. The opponent has previously shown a willingness to branch out from their preferred 'valueF' by offering 'valueC' on issueC and 'valueE' on issueD. I will incorporate both of these opponent signals while dropping my demand on issueA to 'valueB', my second-best preference, to maintain an overall utility around 0.61.

The time is at t=0.80, and the opponent has started showing flexibility on issueC (`valueC`), issueD (`valueE` and `valueF`), and sporadically on issueA and issueB. I will incorporate their preferred values for issueC (`valueC`) and issueD (`valueF`) as these offer me relatively good utility, while securing my high priorities on issueA (`valueA`) and a moderately good value on issueB (`valueB`). This targets a utility of around 0.63, matching two of their known favorable values to push for an agreement before the deadline.

The opponent is consistently starting their search around `valueF` for all issues, but has recently shown some willingness to give `valueC` on `issueC` and `valueE` on `issueD`. With the time at t=0.82, I will concede closer to these revealed preferences on `issueC` and `issueD` to promote a deal, while protecting my high-weight `issueA` at its most valuable level. I will offer `issueB` at `valueC` to provide a reasonable trade-off.

The negotiation is entering the late stage (t=0.84), and the opponent has consistently signaled a strong preference for `valueF` across most issues but recently showed flexibility by conceding `issueC` to `valueC` and occasionally `issueD` to `valueE` or `valueF`. Considering these signals, I will offer `issueC: valueC` and `issueD: valueF`, as both closely align with their recent bids while yielding decent utility for me. To protect my highest-weighted issue, I will hold firm on `issueA: valueA` but make a new concession on `issueB` to `valueD` to offer a novel trade-off.

The opponent has recently revealed a willingness to concede on issueB (offering valueC) and issueC (offering valueC in recent previous rounds). I will combine these concessions by offering issueB: valueC and issueC: valueC, along with issueD: valueF (which the opponent frequently requests). I will hold firm on issueA: valueA, our most important issue, to maintain a strong utility of 0.632 as we approach the final stages of the negotiation.

The deadline is approaching rapidly. The opponent has repeatedly signaled their baseline preference is valueF for most issues, but recently conceded on issueB to valueC, issueC to valueC, and issueA to valueD. I will propose a compromise that adopts their suggested values for B and C, concedes to their baseline preference for D, and steps down to my second best (valueB) on issueA to close the gap while keeping my utility above 0.5.

The deadline is approaching (t=0.90). The opponent has shown willingness to accept 'valueC' for issueB and 'valueC' for issueC, as well as occasionally proposing 'valueD' for issueA. I will offer a combination of these known acceptable values (issueA: valueD, issueB: valueC, issueC: valueC, issueD: valueF) to find a high-probability agreement that still returns a solid utility of approximately 0.47 for me, safely above my reservation value.

The time is currently 0.92, which places heavy pressure on reaching an agreement. The opponent has recently offered issues B, C, and D at values `valueE`, `valueC`, and `valueF`. I am compiling an offer that incorporates `valueC` for `issueB`, `valueE` for `issueC`, and `valueF` for `issueD`—all values they have recently shown a preference for—while slightly securing my own utility (0.525 total) by requesting `valueB` on `issueA`.

The deadline is imminent at t=0.94. I observe that the opponent's last offer included `valueA` for `issueB`, which is my top value for that issue. To maximize the chance of an agreement while maintaining acceptable utility, I will accept their proposed values for `issueA` and `issueB`, and pair them with `valueC` for `issueC` and `valueF` for `issueD`—both of which the opponent has repeatedly offered in recent rounds. This yields a utility of 0.547, securing an agreement above the reservation value.
